review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for concept questions comparing GAN generation with diffusion generation and explaining the role of the diffusion time variable t.

question_bank.question: What is the main conceptual difference between GAN generation and diffusion generation?
choices: GANs use a one-shot generator map, while diffusion uses repeated denoising steps
GANs require labels, while diffusion cannot be conditioned on anything
GANs only work on images, while diffusion only works on text
GANs start from clean data, while diffusion always starts from another real sample
question_bank.explanation: A GAN typically transforms one latent sample directly into an output. Diffusion-style models instead start from noise and refine it through many smaller denoising updates.

question_bank.question: In the diffusion intuition used here, what does the variable t represent?
choices: The noise level, where 0 means clean data and 1 means full noise
The number of classes in the training set
The learning rate used by the optimizer
The latent dimensionality of the generator
question_bank.explanation: The model treats t as a continuum of corruption levels. Small t means the sample is close to clean data, while large t means it is much noisier.
